title: Noory Okthariza
text: You can view my CV here: 👉 (PDF)

title: Tahun Ketiga
text:
Kuliah tahun ketiga saya selesai Mei lalu. Sekarang masuk libur summer. Sebenarnya tidak bisa dibilang libur juga karena banyak urusan riset yang harus dikerjakan. Tapi summer ini saya berkesempatan balik lagi ke Indo. Tujuan utamanya tentu untuk mengumpulkan data, plus bonusnya ketemu dengan teman-teman lama.
Sedikit update, semester kemarin adalah semester terakhir saya ambil mata kuliah. Jadi Fall semester nanti dan seterusnya bisa riang-gembira mengerjakan riset tanpa dibebani tugas-tugas di kelas. Saya lumayan banyak ambil mata kuliah. Total selama enam semester, saya ambil enam belas mata kuliah plus satu mata kuliah reading yang cukup ketemu profesor per dua minggu.
Semester terakhir ini mata kuliah yang saya ambil seru-seru: game theory dan prospectus. Belajar game theory buat saya seperti belajar bahasa baru. Ibaratnya seperti belajar mengerti kata-kata asing, grammar dan hubungan sintaksis supaya bisa ketemu logika dan makna yang valid. Walau cukup kesulitan, menurut saya mata kuliah ini bagus diambil mahasiswa tingkat lanjut, terutama yang ingin - seperti saya - mengonstruksi sebuah teori yang koheren.
Mata kuliah kedua, prospectus, agak unik. Ini mata kuliah yang ditujukan untuk membuat draft proposal disertasi. Hampir setiap minggu mahasiswa dapat giliran presentasi. Format kelasnya seminar, tapi tidak ada bacaan kecuali membaca draft part per part proposal teman-teman di kelas - yang kemudian akan membaca tulisan tersebut dengan sangat attentive. Urutan presentasinya mengikuti format proposal: mulai dari latar belakang dan formulasi pertanyaan penelitian, teori dan hipotesis, strategi empiris, sampai validitas data. Jadi bisa dipastikan hampir setiap minggu setiap mahasiswa kebanjiran kritik dan masukan. Tapi senangnya semua dilakukan dengan semangat kolegial.
Walau draft proposal sudah ada, tapi saya belum bisa mulai secara formal menulis disertasi. Ujian kompre sudah selesai. Tapi saya masih harus menyelesaikan dua paper dan ujian proposal sebelum menyandang status ‘kandidat doktor’. Ritus kuliah PhD di Amerika memang pelan dan mesti dilalui dengan sabar. Idealnya setiap mahasiswa bisa lulus dalam 5 tahun. Tapi bukan hal aneh ada mahasiswa baru selesai dalam 6-7 tahun bahkan lebih.
Karena tidak ada lagi kuliah, Fall nanti saya harus (bisa) kebut menulis dua paper. Satu paper sudah dan sedang saya kerjakan sejak awal summer ini, dan yang satu lagi harusnya bisa dimulai awal Fall. Saya bilang ‘harusnya’ karena paper kedua ini menunggu survei nasional yang rencananya akan dilakukan kolega di CSIS Agustus/September ini. Semoga semua berjalan sesuai rencana. Jadi akhir Fall nanti, atau selambat-lambatnya awal tahun depan, saya bisa ikut ujian proposal.

title: Publications
text:
• 2025. Indonesia election archive: Institutions, candidates and results. (With Khoirunnisa Agustyati, Heroik Pratama, Johanna Birnir, Henry Overos, Iqbal Kholidin, Fadli Ramadhanil, and Amalinda Savirani). Scientific Data, Volume 12: 1397.
• 2025. The patriarchy in the parties: Voter preferences vs. women’s list placement in the 2024 legislative election in Indonesia. (With Johanna Birnir, Khoirunnisa Agustyati, and Heroik Pratama). Electoral Studies, Volume 95, 102938.
• 2024. Islamic political alignment in Indonesia: Does it truly hold?. Asia Policy, 19 (2), 213-217.

title: Noory Okthariza
text:
Noory Okthariza
PhD Student in Political Science, University of Maryland
📍 College Park, MD
✉️ noory@umd.edu
📚 Google Scholar
🆔 ORCID
💼 GitHub




Hi there!
I am a third-year PhD student in Political Science at the University of Maryland. My research focuses on the intricacies of electoral design, party competition, and electoral outcomes. I am also developing a keen interest in understanding patterns of judicial behavior, using causal inference and natural language processing to identify how judges reason, signal, and justify decisions in constitutional and electoral disputes. My work has been published in Scientific Data, Electoral Studies, and Asian Journal of Comparative Politics, as well as in edited volumes by Routledge and Palgrave Macmillan.
Before starting graduate school, I spent over five years working as a researcher at the Jakarta-based think tank, the Centre for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS), where I remain affiliated.
I take caffeine seriously and believe in the endogenous impact of coffee on graduate school success. I’m also a bedroom amateur guitar player who enjoys spending evenings trying to improve my mediocre skills (one riff at a time).



Contact
Department of Government & Politics
University of Maryland
3140 Tydings Hall
College Park, MD 20742
✉️ noory@umd.edu

title: Noory Okthariza
text:
My advisor, our collaborators, and I have been involved in an ongoing effort to compile fine-grained electoral data in Indonesia. The goal is to provide researchers and Indonesian observers with a one-stop, comprehensive resource on Indonesia’s elections. We also work with the election tribunal (KPU) to supplement the datasets we need. So far, we have collected data from 2014 to 2024, and earlier election data will soon be updated as well. Parts of the descriptive analysis of these data have been published in Scientific Data. You can look at the data source here. And here is the abstract of the paper.






NoteAbstract



This paper introduces an Indonesian Election Archive built by a team of scholars and democratic NGO practitioners from Indonesia and the United States. This archive aims to address the calls of international democracy advocates by providing systematic, longitudinal election data across multiple levels of elected administration in Indonesia. We document the efforts of a leading civil society group, the Association for Elections and Democracy (Perludem), and its collaboration with the Indonesian Electoral Commission (KPU) to supply voters with information before and after elections. The archive provides a collection of the laws governing elections to the Presidency and Vice Presidency, the Lower House of the National Legislature (DPR), the Upper House (DPD), regional legislatures and local heads from 1999 to 2024. Additionally, we provide candidate data and election results for the three most recent elections (2014, 2019, and 2024) covering the offices of the Presidency and Vice-Presidency, and both Houses.
